[187:21]From the patriots of Lexington and Concord, to the heroes of Gettysburg and Normandy,

[187:27]from the warriors who crossed the Delaware, to the trailblazers who climbed the Rockies,

[187:33]and from the legends who soared at Kitty Hawk, to the astronauts who touched the moon.

[187:40]Americans have always been the people who defied all

[187:43]arts, transcended all dangers, made the most

[187:47]extraordinary sacrifices, and did whatever it took to

[187:51]defend our children, our country, and our freedom.

[187:55]And as we have seen in this chamber tonight, that same

[187:58]strength, faith, love, and spirit is still alive and

[188:02]thriving in the hearts of the American people.

[188:06]Despite the best efforts of those who would try to censor us, silence us, break us, destroy

[188:11]us, Americans are today a proud, free, sovereign, and independent nation that will always be

[188:20]free and we will fight for it till death.

[188:25]We will never let anything happen to our beloved country.

[188:29]Because we are a country of doers, dreamers, fighters, and survivors.

[188:34]Our ancestors crossed a vast ocean, strode into the unknown wilderness, and carved their

[188:40]fortunes from the rock and soil of a perilous and very dangerous frontier.

[188:47]They chased our destiny across a boundless continent.

[188:52]They built the railroads, laid the highways, and graced the world with American marvels

[188:57]like the Empire State Building, the mighty Hoover Dam, and the towering Golden Gate Bridge.

[189:05]They lit the world with electricity, broke free of the force of gravity, fired up the

[189:16]communists, fascists, and Marxists all over the world,

[189:20]and gave us countless modern wonders sculpted out of iron

[189:23]glass and steel.

[189:26]We stand on the shoulders of these pioneers who won and

[189:29]built the modern age.

[189:31]These workers who poured their sweat into the skylines of

[189:35]our cities.

[189:36]These warriors who shed their blood on fields of battle and

[189:40]gave everything they had for our rights and for our freedom.

[189:45]Now, it is our time to take up the righteous cause of American liberty, and it is our turn

[189:51]to take America's destiny into our own hands and begin the most thrilling days in the history

[189:58]of our country.

[190:00]This will be our greatest era.

[190:03]With God's help over the next four years, we are going to lead this nation even higher

[190:09]And we are going to forge the freest, most advanced, most dynamic, and most dominant

[190:16]civilization ever to exist on the face of this Earth.

[190:21]We are going to create the highest quality of life, build the safest and wealthiest and

[190:32]We are going to conquer the vast frontiers of science,

[190:35]and we're going to lead humanity into space

[190:39]and plant the American flag on the planet Mars

[190:42]and even far beyond.

[191:04]And through it all, we are going to rediscover

[191:06]the unstoppable power of the American spirit.

[191:10]And we are going to renew unlimited promise

[191:13]of the American dream.

[191:15]Every single day, we will stand up

[191:17]and we will fight, fight, fight for the country

[191:20]our citizens believe in

[191:21]and for the country our people deserve.
